message:
harden: sanitize terminal control sequences across display + integrate IssueValidator/CommentValidator + reject MCP ops on tombstones

Three coordinated security/validation hardenings that touch CLI display,
storage, and the MCP server. They are committed together because the
helper exports (`format_type_label`, `format_status_label` reuse,
sanitize_terminal_inline routing) are used across all three layers.

## 1. Comprehensive terminal-escape sanitization

Every user-controlled string that flows into stdout or rich-rendered
output is now passed through `sanitize_terminal_inline` so that
malicious or accidental control sequences in titles, IDs, statuses,
issue types, dep types, labels, reasons, assignees, and owners cannot
move the cursor, clear the screen, or set OSC sequences in the
operator's terminal. Previously, an issue authored with a title like
\"foo\\x1b[2J\" would clear the user's terminal when listed.

New format helpers centralize the sanitization:

  - `format_type_label(IssueType) -> String` (in src/format/text.rs)
    Returns the sanitized text label for an issue type.
  - `format_status_label(&Status, use_color)` now sanitizes its label
    before applying ANSI color, so even Custom(\"...\\x1b...\") variants
    are safe.

Files touched (display path):
  - src/cli/mod.rs (re-exports)
  - src/format/{mod,text,rich}.rs (helper definitions, callsite cleanup,
    new tests for custom statuses/types containing escape bytes)
  - src/cli/commands/{blocked,changelog,count,create,defer,dep,graph,
    lint,ready,reopen,show,stale,update}.rs — each command's plain
    and rich render paths now route IDs, titles, dep types, statuses,
    reasons, assignees, owners, labels, and ref strings through
    sanitize_terminal_inline before printing
  - src/output/components/{dep_tree,issue_panel,issue_table}.rs —
    same routing for the structured rich components

## 2. IssueValidator / CommentValidator integration

Validation now runs at the storage and MCP boundaries, not only in
ad-hoc places.

  - `SqliteStorage::create_issue` invokes `IssueValidator::validate`
    before opening the mutate transaction, returning a
    structured `BeadsError` from validation errors.
  - `SqliteStorage::create_issue` now always computes
    `Issue::compute_content_hash()` and stores it, instead of
    persisting the (possibly stale or absent) `issue.content_hash`
    field — fixes drift between persisted hash and actual content.
  - `SqliteStorage::add_comment` replaces the inline empty-text check
    with `validate_new_comment(...)` which runs `CommentValidator`
    against a synthesized Comment, picking up size limits and any
    other rules added to the validator going forward.
  - In src/mcp/tools.rs:
      * `CreateIssueTool` and `UpdateIssueTool` route titles through
        a single `validate_mcp_title` helper (1..=500 chars after
        trim, multibyte-safe — see new test
        `parse_update_fields_accepts_500_multibyte_character_title`).
      * Both tools route any provided comment through
        `validate_mcp_comment`, which feeds CommentValidator.

## 3. MCP rejects ops against tombstoned issues

`require_valid_issue` previously only checked whether the ID existed.
A tombstoned issue (Status::Tombstone, used to record a deletion
without losing the row) would pass that check, and the caller could
then update / comment / dep-add against it.

Now `require_valid_issue` loads the issue and, if status is
Tombstone, returns a structured `tombstone_issue_err` McpError with
`error_type: TOMBSTONE_ISSUE`, `recoverable: false`, and a hint
pointing the agent at list_issues / create. This protects every MCP
tool that gates on `require_valid_issue` (update, dep, close,
comment, etc.) without per-tool changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
